secretsHandling.note: Stripe restricted keys are best-in-class for fine-grained scoping. Use them — never live full secret keys.

oauthHandling.note: Restricted keys are scoped per-resource and per-permission (Customers:Read, Refunds:Write, etc.).

telemetry.note: Stripe's API request logs apply (governed by Stripe's privacy policy + PCI compliance).

dataResidency.note: Stripe's infrastructure. PCI-regulated for cardholder data.

defaultPermissions.note: Per the restricted key scope. Defaults are narrow if you scope properly.

destructiveTools.note: Refunds and subscription cancellations move real money. Always gate at the host. Pair with a 'never refund > $X' rule in the prompt for unsupervised agents.

promptInjectionExposure.note: Customer descriptions, charge metadata, dispute reason fields can contain user-authored text. Treat as untrusted.

supplyChainSurface.note: Stripe SDK + MCP SDK as direct deps.

rateLimitRisk.note: Stripe rate limits per API key — typically generous, but bulk operations can throttle.

verdictScope: Stripe operations using a properly-scoped restricted key, with destructive tools gated by host-level approval.
verdictNote: Use restricted keys exclusively. Start with read-only scopes; add Write scopes only when you genuinely need them. Test in test mode first. Add a 'never refund > $X' rule for unsupervised agents that have refund authority.
